# Tab overflow affordance: hidden-tab projection on heavy tabs nodes

## Context
ADR 0029 §2.4's projection half (`learn/agentos/decisions/0029-harness-docking-design.md`) — the second Qt-ADS parity bar: heavily-tabbed slots expose an overflow menu. Unfiled per the ADR §5 table. Filed to the delegatable bar: **non-fable implementable.**

## The Problem
When a `tabs` node's headers exceed the available extent, hidden tabs are currently unreachable except by resize. Qt-class docking surfaces the remainder through an overflow control.

## The Architectural Reality
- **Projection affordance, NOT a model change** (ADR verbatim): nothing new persists — `items` order and `activeItemId` already capture the state.
- Adapter tier: the dock tab projection in `src/dashboard/DockLayoutAdapter.mjs`, implemented against `Neo.tab.Container`'s existing header mechanics.
- **Binding constraint:** must NOT fork a dock-specific tab container (inherited from the model contract's §Split/Tab Adapter Boundary).
- Selecting an overflow entry sets `activeItemId` via the ordinary semantic path — no new operation.

## The Fix
One PR: overflow detection on header extent, a dropdown/control listing hidden tabs (title + kind icon per item record), selection routes through the existing active-item path, and the control disappears when everything fits. Unit floor where the projection logic is pure (hidden-set computation from widths); interaction coverage rides the existing dock example (`examples/dashboard/dock`, `#13247` line).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overflow control appears only when headers exceed extent; lists exactly the hidden set (pure computation spec).
- [ ] Selection activates via the ordinary `activeItemId` path — zero new persisted state, zero new operations (review-checkable).
- [ ] No dock-specific tab container fork (review-checkable).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
Grouped drag (`#14770`) · auto-hide rails (§2.7/`#13280` line) · theming polish beyond the landed token layer.

## Related
Parent `#13158` · authority ADR 0029 §2.4. Decision Record impact: aligned-with ADR 0029.

## Intake classification: needs-contract-alignment

I started ticket-intake for #14771 and stopped before branch/code work at the Contract Completeness Gate.

V-B-A performed:
- Live issue body/comments fetched: no Contract Ledger matrix is present.
- KB check: the tab-overflow affordance is valid under #13158 / ADR 0029 and additive on dockZone.v1; no duplicate surfaced in the KB context.
- Memory Core prior-art check: confirms the dock backlog lineage and Qt-ADS parity context, with no competing #14771 implementation claim.
- Current-source sweep: `DockLayoutAdapter` projects `tabs` nodes to `Neo.tab.Container` and ADR 0029 §2.4 explicitly says overflow is a projection affordance, not a model change.
- Contract reference checked: `learn/agentos/process/contract-ledger.md` requires a T3 matrix for consumed surfaces.

Verdict: the premise is plausible, but not intake-ready. This modifies a consumed UI/projection surface (`DockLayoutAdapter` -> `Neo.tab.Container` header mechanics, overflow control behavior, active-item selection path), so ticket-intake forbids guessing the contract without a Contract Ledger matrix.

Required to make this code-ready:

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `DockLayoutAdapter` tabs-node projection | ADR 0029 §2.4 + `learn/agentos/HarnessDockZoneModel.md` | Project an overflow affordance when tab headers exceed available extent | No overflow control when all headers fit; no persisted dockZone mutation | JSDoc if new helper/class is added | Pure hidden-set computation spec + projection review |
| `Neo.tab.Container` header mechanics consumed by dock projection | Current `Neo.tab.Container` surface | Reuse existing tab header mechanics; no dock-specific tab-container fork | If header measurement is unavailable, fail closed to the existing visible tab behavior | None unless public helper/config is added | Unit/static proof against fork/parallel path |
| Overflow entry selection | dockZone.v1 `activeItemId` semantics | Selecting an entry activates the existing item via the ordinary active-item path | Missing item id or stale item record is ignored/rejected without new persisted state | JSDoc/test name | Interaction or unit proof that no new operation/state is introduced |

I added `not-code-ready` + `needs-design` because the repo does not currently expose a more precise `needs-contract-alignment` reason label.
